title: Prewalk via native Fabric trajectory handoff (0.23.0)
approach: Adopt the native pi-fabric 0.23.0 explicit `agents.handoff()` primitive (commit ed08a16) to implement prewalk, replacing the prior synthetic-router design (same slug, superseded — see spec.md 'Superseded approach'). Flip `.pi/fabric.json` `fullCodeMode:false->true` (required for the fabric_exec boundary; isolated to Main per docs/configuration.md:167, children unaffected). Main authors a `fabric_exec` program that does frontier work then calls `agents.handoff({model:'makora/zai-org/GLM-5.2-NVFP4', extensions:true, tools:[read,grep,find,ls,edit,write], thinking:'max'})` — explicit handoff forwards extensions/tools/thinking per call (runRequest:517,536), preserving ADR-009 per-call safety (unlike automatic /fabric prewalk, which inherits subagents.extensions:false and breaks Makora). The handoff is trajectory-preserving (Fabric forks the finalized fabric_exec call+result; the source session is not rewritten) — faithful to the warm-session spirit, deliberately coarser than Stencil/OMP (no TODO gate; Fabric disclaims benchmark equivalence). No custom extension code, no log validator (native carries its own audit/result), no prewalk.model config (explicit handoff carries the model). Operational routing is selective by complexity (prewalk handoff for novel/multi-file M-L; direct Makora agents.run for S/single-file mechanical), both sharing the one blocking-writable-run ceiling from milestone-5. The dogfood (C1) is the first live proof. Ships after milestone-5 settles (shared canonical files; single-writer policy).
blocks: Milestone-5 (active) must settle/commit shared canonical files — especially .pi/prompts/ship.md (uncommitted, milestone-5 B1) — before prewalk /ship edits them (single-writer policy, ADR-002/ADR-009)

Runtime dogfood (C1) is the first live proof; if it fails, the native-handoff adoption is revisited (fallback: two-child planner/executor approximation — explicitly NOT faithful prewalk — or the superseded synthetic router)

fullCodeMode:true is a template-philosophy change (Main authors fabric_exec for all work; Pi core tools move behind pi.* inside fabric_exec) — isolated to Main, children and supervisor unaffected (configuration.md:167)
No automatic /fabric prewalk (would force a global ADR-009 reversal — subagents.extensions:false breaks Makora; rejected)
No prewalk.model config (explicit handoff carries the model per call)
No .pi/settings.json, .opencode/command/*.md, .pi/extensions/, or .pi/tools/ changes (no custom code; native primitive only)
ADR-010 numbering: prewalk reserved it; coordinate with lifecycle-review-gates (next available at write time, 010 or 011)

success_criteria.criterion: ADR-009 default preserved (subagents.extensions still false after the flip)
ADR-010 present (recording native-handoff adoption + fullCodeMode rationale + superseded synthetic-router)
PLAN.md has a canonical prewalk handoff dispatch block with agents.handoff, makora model, extensions:true, thinking:max, exact writable allowlist
AGENTS.md worker topology documents Main full code mode + the prewalk handoff lane (one writable worker, sharing the one-writable-run ceiling, no silent fallback)
success_criteria.verify: rg -qi 'full code mode|fullCodeMode' AGENTS.md && rg -qi 'prewalk|handoff' AGENTS.md

success_criteria.criterion: tech-stack.md documents the fullCodeMode flip + the explicit-handoff route
ship.md encodes selective routing (prewalk handoff for novel/multi-file M-L; direct Makora for S/single-file)
No model regression: all three models resolve after upgrade + flip
Dogfood: handoff result completed === true (first live proof of the lane)
success_criteria.verify: handoff result observed completed === true in the run transcript (C1 checkpoint)

success_criteria.criterion: prd graph bounded: <=3 files per task, 2-3 tasks per child plan, ID-ordered deps

tasks.title: Flip fullCodeMode and record ADR-010 (fabric.json, DECISIONS.md)
tasks.end_state: .pi/fabric.json has fullCodeMode:true and nothing else changed (subagents.extensions still false, defaultTools still read-only — ADR-009 preserved); DECISIONS.md has ADR-010 recording native-handoff adoption, the fullCodeMode rationale, ADR-009 per-call preservation, and the superseded synthetic-router approach.
tasks.verify: jq -e '.fullCodeMode == true and .subagents.extensions == false' .pi/fabric.json && rg -q '^## ADR-010:' .pi/artifacts/pi-template/DECISIONS.md

tasks.title: Canonical prewalk handoff dispatch block (PLAN.md)
tasks.end_state: PLAN.md has a Canonical prewalk handoff dispatch block showing a fabric_exec program that does frontier work then calls agents.handoff({model:'makora/zai-org/GLM-5.2-NVFP4', extensions:true, tools:[read,grep,find,ls,edit,write], thinking:'max'}), beside the direct Makora agents.run block; a prewalk row is added to the dispatch table; selective routing is stated.
tasks.verify: rg -q 'Canonical prewalk handoff dispatch' .pi/artifacts/pi-template/PLAN.md && rg -q 'agents.handoff' .pi/artifacts/pi-template/PLAN.md && rg -q 'makora/zai-org/GLM-5.2-NVFP4' .pi/artifacts/pi-template/PLAN.md && rg -q 'extensions: true' .pi/artifacts/pi-template/PLAN.md

tasks.title: Worker topology and tech-stack documentation (AGENTS.md, tech-stack.md)
tasks.end_state: AGENTS.md worker-topology documents Main full code mode and the prewalk handoff lane (one writable worker, either direct Makora agents.run or Main-authored fabric_exec with explicit handoff to Makora; sharing the one-writable-run ceiling; never overlapping; no silent fallback; ADR-009 per-call preserved); tech-stack.md documents fullCodeMode:true + the explicit-handoff route and notes prewalk.model unused.
tasks.verify: rg -qi 'full code mode|fullCodeMode' AGENTS.md && rg -qi 'prewalk|handoff' AGENTS.md && rg -q 'fullCodeMode' .opencode/tech-stack.md

tasks.title: Selective routing in ship.md (collides with milestone-5 B1 + lifecycle-review-gates B1)
tasks.end_state: ship.md has a Selective Routing section (prewalk handoff for novel/multi-file M-L; direct Makora agents.run for S/single-file mechanical; both sharing the one-writable-run ceiling; never overlapping; no silent fallback) placed after Host-Derived Candidate Intake and before Checkpoint Protocol. All existing ship.md content (including milestone-5 host-derived intake) preserved. Sequenced after milestone-5 B1 settles.

tasks.title: Reload checkpoint and dogfood the handoff lane
tasks.end_state: After a human /reload or Pi restart, pi --approve --list-models resolves all three models; one Main-authored fabric_exec program performs frontier work, an edit, and an explicit agents.handoff() to Makora that completes a real desired edit; the handoff result shows handedOff:true, completed:true. First end-to-end live proof of the lane.
tasks.verify: pi --approve --list-models | grep -F 'makora/zai-org/GLM-5.2-NVFP4' (handoff result completed === true observed in the run transcript)

tasks.title: No-regression and model resolution (verify, no-write)
tasks.end_state: All success-criteria checks pass read-only: fullCodeMode:true, ADR-009 default preserved, ADR-010 present, PLAN handoff block present, AGENTS/tech-stack/ship updated, all three models resolve, .pi/fabric.json parses under 0.23.0, Main settings untouched. No silent fallback anywhere in the docs.
tasks.verify: full success-criteria suite runs green; git diff --check
